title: Classify Heim-PC wide listeners
goal: Classify and, where appropriate, narrow wide Heim-PC listeners including Unifi, Sunshine, GNOME Remote Desktop, Leitstand and Caddy.

approval.note: User authorized full audit remediation. Stop only proven unmanaged previews; change Leitstand runtime only after reviewed code, green CI, preserved unit/log evidence and loopback HTTP rollback proof.

acceptance.assertion: Every listener on 0.0.0.0 or * has an owner, purpose, expected network scope and exposure boundary.
Unmanaged preview servers are either stopped or moved behind explicit local-only/dev-only commands.
Any port binding or firewall change is made only after preserving required LAN/Tailnet functions.

Leitstand server code defaults to loopback and rejects wildcard binding unless an explicit unsafe-wide-bind acknowledgement is present; focused tests cover default, explicit LAN IP, invalid host and wildcard acknowledgement.
The live Leitstand user service is rebuilt from merged main, explicitly configured for 127.0.0.1, and post-restart proof shows HTTP success on loopback with no wildcard listener on port 3000.
Ports 4173 and 4174 are closed with process/unit provenance and clean-repository evidence; no parent tmux shell or worktree is deleted.

metadata.live_audit_finding: unmanaged commonworld http.server on 0.0.0.0:4173 was stopped; remaining wide listeners need policy classification.
metadata.scope_update_20260711: Live audit identified unmanaged Commonworld and merged-Weltgewebe preview listeners on 4173/4174 plus Leitstand contract drift on *:3000. The preview listeners were closed without deleting shells/worktrees; Leitstand requires a repo-bound safe-default patch and runtime proof.
validation: All observed wildcard listener families were assigned to an owner, purpose, expected network scope and decision in the Infra runtime evidence.
Ports 4173 and 4174 were closed after process/unit attribution; their parent tmux shell and worktree were retained.
Leitstand PR #161 passed 341 tests, build, drift, security, AI-context and Cloudflare checks on its exact head.
Leitstand was deployed from immutable merge release 99a925cba0b71ddfba7acc85367930e90e119b77 and returned HTTP 200 with health status ok.
Port 3000 is bound only to 127.0.0.1; ports 4173 and 4174 remained closed.
SSH, Tailscale, Caddy, UniFi, Sunshine, GNOME Remote Desktop, Avahi, libvirt and cups-browsed remained active after the bounded changes.
Infra PR #116 validated and published the redacted listener/runtime evidence.

verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-1: Infra runtime evidence classifies every observed wildcard listener family by owner, purpose, expected scope and explicit retain/narrow/close decision.
verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-2: Obsolete preview listeners 4173/4174 were closed and required operator services remained active; no parent shell or worktree was deleted.
verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-3: The evidence distinguishes loopback, LAN, virtual-network and overlay purposes and explicitly leaves router forwarding, complete firewall policy and categorical public ingress unverified.
verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-4: Leitstand PR #161 implements IP-literal bind validation, loopback default, explicit wildcard acknowledgement and focused default/LAN/invalid/wildcard tests.
verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-5: The live service runs the exact merged release with LEITSTAND_BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1, health status ok and no wildcard port-3000 listener.
verified_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t004-6: Local listener evidence binds 4173 to the unmanaged Commonworld process and 4174 to the merged-Weltgewebe transient unit; both are closed while shell/worktree state is retained.

metadata.outcome: All observed Heim-PC wildcard listener families are classified. Obsolete previews 4173/4174 are closed; Leitstand is healthy and loopback-only on 127.0.0.1:3000; retained operator services remain active. Router forwarding, complete firewall policy and categorical absence of public ingress remain explicitly unverified.
metadata.self_review: PASS: verification is bound to Bureau PR #410, Leitstand PR #161, Infra PR #116, exact heads/merges, final diff hashes and local runtime evidence hashes. It does not claim firewall correctness, public-ingress absence, service necessity or permission to disable retained services.
